sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see 4 white corner pieces at positions [17,17], [17,44], [26,17], [26,46]. These are the corners of the red box.
Let me reconsider the problem. The connection slot is light green (N). The top row has N as the 5th color. Maybe the puzzle is not about matching the top row order exactly, but about something else.
Let me look at level 1 more carefully. In level 1, the top row had b, N, Y, p and the completion state also had b, N, Y, p. The palette had N, p, b, Y. So the player needed to rearrange the palette to match the top row.
In level 2, the top row is O, p, R, b, N, Y, M. The palette is R, p, N, O, M, b, Y. The current arrangement is Red=[O, p, R], Green=[b, N, Y, M] which reads as O, p, R, b, N, Y, M - matching the top row!
But SPACE isn't working. Maybe the issue is that the connection slot has a green highlight, meaning something is still selected. Let me try clicking somewhere neutral to deselect, then SPACE.
Or maybe I need to click on the connection slot itself to "activate" the connection.
Actually, wait - let me look at the image more carefully. The connection slot shows a green square with a border. This might be the "active" slot. Maybe I need to fill this slot with a color too?
But there are only 7 colors and 7 slots (3 in red + 4 in green). The connection slot is separate.
Hmm, let me try a different approach. Maybe the order should be read differently. What if the red box colors should match the first 3 colors of the top row, and the green box should match the last 4?
Top row: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
Red box target: O, p, R ✓
Green box target: b, N, Y, M ✓
This matches! So why isn't SPACE working?
Let me try clicking on the green connection slot to deselect it, then try SPACE.

action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 35},  # click connection slot to deselect
    {'action': 'SPACE'},
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
